steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between Lady in Brown and Mobile: Pittsburgh. The correct choice must say something true of both artworks that highlights what they share.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Both Lady in Brown and Mobile: Pittsburgh are Carnegie Prize gold medal-winning artworks.: Directly states a shared characteristic of both artworks — both won the Carnegie Prize gold medal. ✓
  • Choice B — Lavery, a painter, won a Carnegie Prize gold medal in 1896 for Lady in Brown.: Mentions only one artwork and one artist. It describes Lavery alone, not any similarity between the two works. ✗
  • Choice C — The Carnegie Museum of Art in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, has awarded Carnegie Prize gold medals to both painters and sculptors.: Focuses on the museum's scope across artistic disciplines, not on a similarity between the two specific artworks. ✗
  • Choice D — In contrast to Lady in Brown, a painting, Mobile: Pittsburgh is a sculpture.: Explicitly signals a difference (medium: painting vs. sculpture), which is the opposite of a similarity. ✗
That is the correct answer.
